capability_objective: Plan and execute independent lookups in parallel, gate dependent retrievals on uniquely resolved stable IDs, respect read-only negative constraints, and synthesize an exact-format comparison from full records.
prompt: The events lead is checking lodging holds referenced in two venue notes. Work only in this repository and use the genuine local lodging-desk executable for all reservation access; do not inspect or query its seed data or runtime directory directly. The executable is `python3 lodging_desk.py`; its built-in help describes the available commands. Find the reservation named “Orchard Room Leadership Retreat” at “Austin” and the reservation named “Juniper Suite Planning Stay” at “Raleigh”. Your first reservation-data shell execution must start both independent name-and-location searches concurrently from that one shell execution and wait for both. After both results return, retrieve a branch only when its search produced exactly one stable ID, using only that returned ID. When both branches resolve, your next reservation-data shell execution must start both independent full-record retrievals concurrently from that one shell execution and wait for both. Compare the status values from the full records, not from search snippets. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Write the result to `lodging-audit.md`. That file must contain only a Markdown table with the exact header `| Record | ID | Date | Status |`, the separator `|---|---|---|---|`, and exactly two data rows in the requested order. Format every data row exactly as `| {Record} | {ID} | {Date} | {Status} |`, substituting the full-record name, stable ID, date, and status verbatim. End the final data row with exactly one newline; include no heading, blank line, or prose.
